---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
author: "Alex L. Jones"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM YYYY"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - margin=2.2cm
    fontsize: 10pt
    linkcolor: "black"
    urlcolor: "black"
    colorlinks: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \titleformat{\section}{\Large\bfseries\sffamily}{}{0em}{}[\titlerule]
        \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{1.4em}{0.6em}
        \titleformat{\subsection}{\large\bfseries\sffamily}{}{0em}{}
        \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{0.9em}{0.3em}
        \setlist[itemize]{leftmargin=1.2em, itemsep=2pt, topsep=2pt}
        \pagenumbering{arabic}
---

```{=latex}
\noindent
{\Large\textbf{Alex L. Jones, PhD}}\\
Associate Professor of Psychological Data Science\\
School of Psychology, Swansea University\\[0.5em]
\href{mailto:alex.l.jones@swansea.ac.uk}{alex.l.jones@swansea.ac.uk}\\
\href{https://alexjonesphd.github.io}{alexjonesphd.github.io}\\
\href{https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3600-3644}{ORCID: 0000-0003-3600-3644}\\
\href{https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=ddZUIYEAAAAJ}{Google Scholar}
```

# Research interests

Face perception; facial morphometrics and geometric morphometric methods; first impressions and social trait inference; Bayesian inference and hierarchical modelling; psychometrics and latent-variable modelling; psychological data science.

# Education

**PhD, Psychology** — Bangor University, 2010–2013. Pass with no corrections.

**BSc (Hons), Psychology** — Bangor University, 2007–2010. First Class.

# Employment

**Associate Professor of Psychological Data Science** — School of Psychology, Swansea University, 2025–present.

**Senior Lecturer in Psychology** — Swansea University, 2020–2025.

**Lecturer in Psychology** — Swansea University, 2016–2020.

**Postdoctoral Research Associate** — Gettysburg College, USA, 2014–2015.

**Postdoctoral Research Associate** — Bangor University, 2013–2014.

# Teaching

## Module convenership

**PSY334 — Evolutionary Perspectives on Psychology**, Swansea University. Module lead. Final year undergraduate module.

**PSM-99 - Quantitative Perspectives on Psychology - from Measurement to Models**, Swansea University. Module Lead. MSc Research Methods module.

## Online courses (open access)

**Data Analysis with Python** — \href{https://alexjonesphd.github.io/python_data_analysis/}{alexjonesphd.github.io/python\_data\_analysis}. A free introduction to the Python scientific stack for social-science researchers.

**Statistical Modelling with Python** — \href{https://alexjonesphd.github.io/statistical_models_python/}{alexjonesphd.github.io/statistical\_models\_python}. A more advanced course on building and interpreting statistical models in Python, including Bayesian methods with PyMC.

## Teaching qualifications

PGCertHE / Fellow of AdvanceHE (FHEA), 2018.

# Research supervision

**PhD** — Three students supervised to completion in last 5 years.

**MSc thesis supervision** — 3–6 students per year over the past decade.

**BSc dissertation supervision** — 7–10 students per year over the past decade.

# Service

## Grant review

**Grant panel member**, Research Foundation – Flanders (FWO), 2024–2025.

**Ad hoc grant reviewer** — Economic and Social Research Council (ESRC, UK); Czech Science Foundation.

## Editorial roles

PLOS ONE - 2019-2023
Evolution and Human Behaviour - 2022-Present

# Professional memberships

Experimental Psychology Society.

# Industry consulting

Consulting and applied research collaborations with industry partners on behavioural and psychological data, including Boots, Chanel, CyDen Ltd, eharmony, and Vanquis.

# Technical skills

**Programming** — Python (advanced; primary research language), R.

**Probabilistic programming** — PyMC (primary), with experience in Stan and bambi.

**Statistical methods** — Bayesian hierarchical modelling, structural equation modelling, item response theory, generalised linear and mixed-effects models, geometric morphometric methods, multivariate methods.

**Open Science** — All recent work shared via OSF (\href{https://osf.io/v3p5g}{osf.io/v3p5g}); code on GitHub (\href{https://github.com/alexjonesphd}{github.com/alexjonesphd}).

# Publications

PDFs and DOIs available at \href{https://alexjonesphd.github.io/publications.html}{alexjonesphd.github.io/publications}.}

## 2026

- Jaeger, B., **Jones, A. L.**, Satchell, L., Schild, C., & van Leeuwen, F. (2026). Who likes extraverts? Testing the interplay between perceiver needs and target appearance in impression formation. *International Review of Social Psychology*, 39(1): 2, 1–13.

## 2025

- Satchell, L. P., Hall, J., & **Jones, A. L.** (2025). Do we look at a threatening person's face? The relationship between perception and observation of walking strangers. *Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*.
- Kramer, R. S. S., **Jones, A. L.**, Fitousi, D., & Tree, J. J. (2025). AI-generated images of familiar faces are indistinguishable from real photographs. *Cognitive Research: Principles and Implications*, 10(70).
- **Jones, A. L.**, Kordsmeyer, T. L., Kramer, R. S. S., Stern, J., & Penke, L. (2025). Updating evidence on facial metrics: A Bayesian perspective on sexual dimorphism in facial width-to-height ratio and bizygomatic width. *Evolution and Human Behavior*, 46, 106781.
- Rayner, C. L., Davies, J., Bradshaw, C. A., & **Jones, A. L.** (2025). Patterns of recreational substance use, help seeking and harm reduction among UK music festival attendees. *Drug Science, Policy and Law*, 11, 1–12.
- Kannan, C., **Jones, A. L.**, Towler, J., & Tree, J. J. (2025). Do young and older adult populations perform equivalently across different automatic face-trait judgements? Evidence for differential impacts of ageing. *PLOS ONE*, 20(5), e0322165.
- Kramer, R. S. S., **Jones, A. L.**, & Fitousi, D. (2025). Face familiarity and similarity: Within- and between-identity representations are altered by learning. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception & Performance*.
- Tree, J. J., Kramer, R. S. S., & **Jones, A. L.** (2025). Upright and inverted unfamiliar face-matching tasks — everything correlates everywhere all at once. *Memory & Cognition*.
- Tree, J. J., & **Jones, A. L.** (2025). Exploring insight into unfamiliar face recognition ability: The case from developmental prosopagnosia. *Cortex*.
- Tree, J. J., & **Jones, A. L.** (2025). How prevalent is "other ethnicity blindness"? Exploring the extremes of recognition performance across categories of faces. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: General*.
- James, M. L., Stiles, V. H., von Lieres un Wilkau, H. C., **Jones, A.**, Willy, R. W., Ashford, K. J., & Moore, I. S. (2025). Predictors of pelvic acceleration during treadmill running across various stride frequency conditions. *Sports Biomechanics*, 1–15.

## 2024

- Kramer, R. S. S., Javorkova, N., & **Jones, A.** (2024). No influence of face familiarity on the cheerleader effect. *Visual Cognition*, 1–11.
- **Jones, A.**, Shiramizu, V., & Jones, B. C. (2024). Decoding the language of first impressions: Comparing models of first impressions of faces derived from free-text descriptions and trait ratings. *British Journal of Psychology*, 1–16.
- Kramer, R. S. S., Ritchie, K. L., Flack, T. R., Mireku, M. O., & **Jones, A.** (2024). The psychometrics of rating facial attractiveness using different response scales. *Perception*, 1–16.
- Jaeger, B., Sleegers, W. W. A., Stern, J., Penke, L., & **Jones, A.** (2024). Testing perceivers' accuracy and accuracy awareness when forming personality impressions from faces. *European Journal of Personality*, 1–21.

## 2023

- Kramer, R. S. S., Jarvis, J.-L., Green, M., & **Jones, A.** (2023). The relationship between facial attractiveness and perceived guilt across crime type. *Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, 1–9.
- Satchell, L. P., Jaeger, B., **Jones, A.**, López, B., & Schild, C. (2023). Beyond reliability in first impressions research: Considering validity and the need to "mix it up with folks". *Social Psychological Bulletin*, 18, e10211.
- Kramer, R. S. S., Flanagan, E., **Jones, A.**, & Gous, G. (2023). Wisdom of the inner crowd benefits both face and voice matching. *Applied Cognitive Psychology*, 1–9.
- Turner, A. D., Ong, J. C., **Jones, A.**, Tu, A., Salanitro, & Crawford, M. R. (2023). Neurocognitive functioning in comorbid insomnia and sleep apnea patients is better after positive airway pressure therapy, but worse after cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia. *Sleep*, 46, 1–12.
- Bobak, A. K., **Jones, A.**, Hilker, Z., Mestry, N., Bate, S., & Hancock, P. J. B. (2023). Data-driven studies in face identity processing rely on the quality of the tests and data sets. *Cortex*, 166, 348–364.
- Embling, R., Price, M. J., Lee, M. D., **Jones, A.**, & Wilkinson, L. L. (2023). Associations between dietary variety, portion size and body weight: Prospective evidence from UK Biobank participants. *British Journal of Nutrition*, 1–11.
- Hadden, L. M., Penny, H., **Jones, A.**, Partridge, A. M., Lancaster, T. M., & Allen, C. (2023). Pre-frontal stimulation does not reliably increase reward responsiveness. *Cortex*, 159, 268–285.

## 2022

- Batres, C., **Jones, A.**, Barlett, C. P., Porcheron, A., Morizot, F., & Russell, R. (2022). Makeup works by modifying factors of facial beauty. *Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts*.
- Kramer, R. S. S., & **Jones, A.** (2022). Incomplete faces are completed using a more average face. *Cognitive Research: Principles and Implications*, 7:708.
- Childs, M. J., & **Jones, A.** (2022). Perceptions of individuals who engage in age concealment. *Evolutionary Behavioral Sciences*.
- Ong, J., Dawson, S., Taylor, H., Park, M., Burgess, H., Crawford, M., Rains, J., Smitherman, T., Espie, C., **Jones, A.**, & Wyatt, J. (2022). A micro-longitudinal study of naps, sleep disturbance, and headache severity in women with chronic migraine. *Behavioural Sleep Medicine*.
- Jaeger, B., & **Jones, A.** (2022). Which facial features are central in impression formation? *Social Psychological and Personality Science*, 13(2), 553–561.

## 2021

- **Jones, A.** (2021). Height shows no clear association with self-serving beliefs about wealth redistribution: A commentary on Richardson (2020). *Evolution and Human Behaviour*, 42, 593–596.
- Kramer, R., & **Jones, A.** (2021). Wanting or having children predicts age preferences in online dating. *Evolutionary Behavioral Sciences*.
- Holzleitner, I., **Jones, A.**, O'Shea, K., Cassar, R., Fasolt, V., Shiramizu, V., Jones, B., & DeBruine, L. (2021). Do 3D face images capture cues of strength, weight, and height better than 2D face images do? *Adaptive Human Behavior and Physiology*, 7(3), 209–219.
- Childs, M., **Jones, A.**, Thwaites, P., Zdravković, S., Thorley, C., Suzuki, A., Shen, R., Ding, Q., Burns, E., Xu, H., & Tree, J. (2021). Do individual differences in face recognition ability moderate the other ethnicity effect? *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance*, 47(7), 893–907.
- Moore, I., James, M., Brockwell, E., Perkins, J., **Jones, A.**, & Donnelly, G. (2021). Multidisciplinary, biopsychosocial factors contributing to return to running and running related stress urinary incontinence in postpartum women. *British Journal of Sports Medicine*, 55(22).
- **Jones, A.**, & Kramer, R. (2021). Facial first impressions form two clusters representing approach-avoidance. *Cognitive Psychology*, 126, 101387.
- **Jones, A.**, Schild, C., & Jones, B. (2021). Facial metrics generated from manually and automatically placed image landmarks are highly correlated. *Evolution and Human Behavior*, 42(3), 186–193.
- Kramer, R., **Jones, A.**, & Gous, G. (2021). Individual differences in face and voice matching abilities: The relationship between accuracy and consistency. *Applied Cognitive Psychology*, 35(1), 192–202.
- **Jones, A.**, Jaeger, B., & Schild, C. (2021). No credible evidence for links between 2D:4D and COVID-19 outcomes: A probabilistic perspective on digit ratio, ACE variants, and national case fatalities. *Early Human Development*, 152, 105272.

## 2020

- Kramer, R., & **Jones, A.** (2020). Sequential effects in facial attractiveness judgments using cross-classified models: Investigating perceptual and response biases. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance*, 46(12), 1476–1489.
- Kramer, R., Berry, C., **Jones, A.**, & Gous, G. (2020). Face familiarity and image-specific memory. *Perception*, 49(9).
- Crawford, M., Luik, A., Espie, C., Taylor, H., Burgess, H., **Jones, A.**, & Ong, J. (2020). Digital cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia in women with chronic migraines. *Headache: The Journal of Head and Face Pain*, 60(5), 902–915.

## 2019

- Callow, N., Edwards, M., **Jones, A.**, Hardy, L., & Connell, S. (2019). Action dual tasks reveal differential effects of visual imagery perspectives on motor performance. *Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, 72(6), 1401–1411.
- Russell, R., Batres, C., **Jones, A.**, & Porcheron, A. (2019). A role for contrast gain control in skin appearance. *Journal of Vision*, 19(11).
- Blagrove, M., Hale, S., Lockheart, J., Carr, M., **Jones, A.**, & Valli, K. (2019). Testing the empathy theory of dreaming: The relationships between dream sharing and trait and state empathy. *Frontiers in Psychology*, 10, 1351.
- **Jones, A.**, Tree, J., & Ward, R. (2019). Personality in faces: Implicit associations between appearance and personality. *European Journal of Social Psychology*, 49(3), 658–669.
- **Jones, A.**, & Jaeger, B. (2019). Biological bases of beauty revisited: The effect of symmetry, averageness, and sexual dimorphism on female facial attractiveness. *Symmetry*, 11(2), 279.

## 2018

- **Jones, A.** (2018). The influence of shape and colour cue classes on facial health perception. *Evolution and Human Behavior*, 39(1), 19–29.

## 2017

- **Jones, A.**, Porcheron, A., & Russell, R. (2017). Makeup changes the apparent size of facial features. *Psychology of Aesthetics, Creativity, and the Arts*.
- **Jones, A.**, Batres, C., Porcheron, A., Sweda, J., Morizot, F., & Russell, R. (2017). Positive facial affect looks healthy. *Visual Cognition*, 1–12.
- Mason, K., Barlett, C., & **Jones, A.** (2017). The influence of the opposite sex on hypothetical aggressive inclinations. *Journal of Interpersonal Violence*, 088626051772483.
- Russell, R., Kramer, S., & **Jones, A.** (2017). Facial contrast declines with age but remains sexually dimorphic throughout adulthood. *Adaptive Human Behavior and Physiology*.
- Sumanapala, D., Fish, L., **Jones, A.**, & Cross, E. (2017). Have I grooved to this before? Discriminating practised and observed actions in a novel context. *Acta Psychologica*, 175, 42–49.

## 2016

- Diersch, N., **Jones, A.**, & Cross, E. (2016). The timing and precision of action prediction in the aging brain. *Human Brain Mapping*, 37(1), 54–66.
- Mileva, V., **Jones, A.**, Russell, R., Little, A., & Jones, A. (2016). Sex differences in the perceived dominance and prestige of women with and without cosmetics. *Perception*, 45(10), 1166–1183.
- **Jones, A.**, & Kramer, R. (2016). Facial cosmetics and attractiveness: Comparing the effect sizes of professionally-applied cosmetics and identity. *PLOS ONE*, 11(10), e0164218.
- Russell, R., Porcheron, A., Sweda, J., **Jones, A.**, Mauger, E., & Morizot, F. (2016). Facial contrast is a cue for perceiving health from the face. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance*, 42(9), 1354–1362.
- **Jones, A.**, Porcheron, A., Sweda, J., Morizot, F., & Russell, R. (2016). Coloration in different areas of facial skin is a cue to health: The role of cheek redness and periorbital luminance in health perception. *Body Image*, 17, 57–66.

## 2015

- Kramer, R., & **Jones, A.** (2015). Do people's first names match their faces? *Journal of Articles in Support of the Null Hypothesis*, 12(1).
- Scott, N., **Jones, A.**, Kramer, R., & Ward, R. (2015). Facial dimorphism in autistic quotient scores. *Clinical Psychological Science*, 3(2), 230–241.
- **Jones, A.**, & Kramer, R. (2015). Facial cosmetics have little effect on attractiveness judgments compared with identity. *Perception*, 44(1), 79–86.
- **Jones, A.**, Russell, R., & Ward, R. (2015). Cosmetics alter biologically-based factors of beauty: Evidence from facial contrast. *Evolutionary Psychology*, 13(1).

## 2014

- **Jones, A.**, Kramer, R., & Ward, R. (2014). Miscalibrations in judgements of attractiveness with cosmetics. *The Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology*, 67(10), 2060–2068.

## 2013

- Kramer, R., **Jones, A.**, & Sharma, D. (2013). Sequential effects in judgements of attractiveness: The influences of face race and sex. *PLOS ONE*, 8(12), e82226.
- Scott, N., Kramer, R., **Jones, A.**, & Ward, R. (2013). Facial cues to depressive symptoms and their associated personality attributions. *Psychiatry Research*, 208(1), 47–53.

## 2012

- **Jones, A.**, Kramer, R., & Ward, R. (2012). Signals of personality and health: The contributions of facial shape, skin texture, and viewing angle. *Journal of Experimental Psychology: Human Perception and Performance*, 38(6), 1353–1361.
- Kramer, R., **Jones, A.**, & Ward, R. (2012). A lack of sexual dimorphism in width-to-height ratio in white european faces using 2D photographs, 3D scans, and anthropometry. *PLOS ONE*, 7(8), e42705.

\vfill
\begin{center}
\small\textit{This CV is generated from a Quarto source file. Last updated: \today.}
\end{center}
